description: PR4 scenario #2B — prayer-rule divergence: legacy _start_is_blocked filter rejects a slot the configured-windows rule permits. A candidate at 12:00 on an exception day with NO configured prayer window should be permitted by the configured rule but blocked by the hardcoded 11:35–12:59 legacy filter. The commit-4 divergence report records this as a 'legacy \ configured' case.
notes: Commit 4 turns this fixture into numeric rows in the divergence report.
This is a 'legacy kills, configured permits' case — proves the configured rule is not a superset of the legacy filter.
Together with divergence_a.json, these two fixtures force the bidirectional set-difference view, rebutting any 'one is a subset' assumption.
